items.why: Students confuse fat- and water-soluble vitamins. Mnemonic: ADEK fat-soluble (stored in body fat - can build up to toxic levels). B and C water-soluble (excess excreted in urine).

items.q: Why does food provenance matter to consumers?
items.correct: It affects environmental impact, quality, ethics, and supports local economies.
items.random_wrong: It tells you the calorie content.
items.why: Students reduce food choice to price. Provenance (origin) raises questions of food miles, fair trade, animal welfare, seasonal availability, and supporting local producers - all consumer concerns.

items.q: What are the 4 Cs of food hygiene?
items.correct: Cleaning, Cooking, Chilling, and avoiding Cross-contamination.
items.why: Students guess at the Cs. They specifically address the four food-safety risks: dirty surfaces, undercooked food, warm storage, and pathogens transferring between foods (raw to ready-to-eat).

items.q: Bread is made with which flour?
items.why: Correct answer: Strong. “Self-raising” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: CO2. “O2” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The real figure is 4. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
